behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality: whether the vision model actually classifies explicit content correctly. A stubbed classifier would always return the expected flag and hide real misses. Rejected Workflow because behavior text is a single model-driven moderation decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real moderation on a curated explicit-image set through the production vision model path; assert each sample is marked flagged, routed to hold-for-review, and not sent to publish.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This explicitly evaluates robustness of model output quality under adversarial text variants. Deterministic mocks cannot expose failures in real hate-speech detection with obfuscation. Rejected Workflow because this is one classification step, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants to the production text moderation model path; verify outputs are flagged and held, including unicode/lookalike substitutions.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is module handoff/orchestration: combining image/text pass results and executing publish state transition without drop/hold regressions. This can fail when service, decision logic, and persistence/publish modules are wired together. Rejected Agentic because behavior text does not require validating model quality, only routing when checks already pass.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service + decision engine + repository/publisher with deterministic pass results from both check modules; assert final state is published and no hold/drop side effects occur.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO across real pipeline execution under throughput pressure, where real process/network behavior and runtime contention matter. In-process mocks can understate latency and hide queueing/timeouts. Rejected Workflow because requirement is timing of pipeline completion, not correctness of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end load test against deployed service path (submission ingress through final decision) with real network/process boundaries and representative traffic; assert p95/p99 completion stays within 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure is error-handling/state-propagation across local modules (vision client error -> moderation decision -> retry queue). A controlled unavailable response from a fake/stub client can reproduce this exactly. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network semantics beyond propagating an unavailability condition.
behaviors.test_description: Inject vision-client unavailability errors in an integrated service test and verify image posts are enqueued with retry metadata, not marked approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency on borderline content depends on real model behavior (and any model-serving nondeterminism), which mocks cannot reveal. The same image must be judged repeatedly by the real vision path to catch variance. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated evaluation of one model decision type, not a chained multi-agent decision pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Resubmit identical borderline images multiple times within the defined short window against the real vision moderation path; measure decision variance and assert consistency threshold/policy is met.
